title: fix: switch flux provider from fireworks to vast.ai
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Flux has run primarily on Vast.ai RTX 5090s since 2026-07-02 (#12171), with Fireworks now only a fallback path. The registry still listed fireworks as the provider.

- registry \`provider\` field: fireworks -> vast
- drop the stale "(Fireworks)" attribution from the description

gist.headline: Registry Reality Check
gist.blurb: Updated our internal registry to reflect that Flux is now primarily running on Vast.ai's RTX 5090s instead of Fireworks.
gist.summary: Updated the image registry metadata to correctly list Vast.ai as the primary provider for Flux models instead of Fireworks.
gist.impact: The API and documentation will now correctly attribute Flux generation to Vast.ai, matching our actual infrastructure routing.
gist.visual_concept: The bee (Polly) updating a label or sign on a giant, glowing server rack (representing Vast.ai RTX 5090s), peeling off an old sticker to reveal the new provider.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot peeling an old sticker off a massive, glowing retro server rack and replacing it with a new one. The server rack emits a soft lime green (#ecf874) glow. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot peeling an old sticker off a massive, glowing retro server rack and replacing it with a new one. The server rack emits a soft lime green (#ecf874) glow. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
